# Claude Teacher — Auto-generated Project Explanation

For every project, when the user finishes a significant piece of work (feature, refactoring, bug fix), write a detailed `FOR_YOUNGWOO.md` file in the project root that explains the whole project in plain language.

## What to include

- The technical architecture and how the codebase is structured
- How the various parts are connected to each other
- The technologies used and why those technical decisions were made
- Lessons learned: bugs encountered and how they were fixed, potential pitfalls and how to avoid them, best practices discovered
- How good engineers think and work — practical insights from the implementation process

## Writing style

- Make it very engaging to read. Do not make it sound like boring technical documentation or a textbook.
- Use analogies and anecdotes to make it more understandable and memorable.
- Write as if explaining to a smart colleague who is new to the project.

## When to generate

- When the user explicitly asks for it
- When a major feature or refactoring is completed and the user runs `/ve-project-recap`
- Do NOT generate automatically on every small change — only on significant milestones or when asked
